texto: This explained to me--and I suppose, forgave me--my inability to see the face of this man, because whoever must deceive us in order to live will by necessity far exceed the skill of ordinary men, who are as much tempted by the desire to be honest as they are plagued by guilt and shame when they have broken faith.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Michael Ennis sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “This explained to me--and I suppose, forgave me--my inability to see the face of this man, becaus…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a fe, deception, necessary-evil, útil em momentos de necessary-evil.
